items.titleZh: 以读取为中心的DTCO应用于IGZO铁电场效应晶体管的3D异构AI存储器（imec、鲁汶大学）
items.descriptionZh:
比利时imec微电子研究中心与鲁汶大学的研究团队近日在《半导体工程》期刊上发表了题为“DTCO of NOR-Type IGZO FeFETs for 3D Heterogeneous AI Memories: A Read-Centric Perspective”的研究论文。该工作从以读取为中心的设计-技术协同优化（DTCO）视角出发，系统评估了NOR型IGZO铁电场效应晶体管（FeFET）在三维异构人工智能存储器中的适用性。

**背景与上下文**：随着AI计算对存储带宽和能效的需求激增，传统冯·诺依曼架构面临严重的“存储墙”瓶颈。三维异构集成技术通过将存储器与逻辑芯片垂直堆叠或混合键合，有望解决这一问题。IGZO（铟镓锌氧化物）FeFET作为一种新型非易失性存储器件，兼具氧化物半导体的低温后道工序（BEOL）集成能力和铁电材料的快速开关特性，特别适合用于构建高密度、低功耗的嵌入式存储器。然而，现有研究多聚焦于写入性能，忽略了AI推理中读取操作占主导的特点（读取次数远多于写入）。为此，该研究提出了以读取为中心的设计优化策略。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：研究团队采用了NOR型阵列架构——在这种结构中，每个存储单元采用FeFET作为存取管，源极和漏极通过位线连接，栅极通过字线控制，形成类似NOR闪存的并行读取路径。与传统NAND型相比，NOR型在随机读取延迟上具有天然优势（可低至纳秒级），但单元面积较大（约6-8 F²）。创新点在于：1）将IGZO作为FeFET沟道材料，利用其高迁移率（约10-30 cm²/Vs）和低温（<400°C）沉积特性，实现与CMOS后道工艺兼容的BEOL集成，从而在逻辑芯片上方直接堆叠存储层，缩短数据路径；2）采用读取中心DTCO方法，系统优化了铁电材料（如HfZrOx）的极化切换特性、沟道厚度和栅极电压，在保证读取裕度（sense margin）的同时，将读取干扰（read disturb）降至最低；3）针对三种集成场景分别建模：片上BEOL RAM（嵌入逻辑芯片内）、混合键合存储器芯粒（通过Cu-Cu键合堆叠）以及片外独立存储器（如3D DRAM）。

**性能参数与对比数据**：论文通过TCAD仿真和紧凑模型分析了关键指标。在BEOL集成场景下，NOR型IGZO FeFET的读取延迟约为5-10 ns（相比传统SRAM的亚纳秒级仍有一定差距，但远优于NAND闪存的微秒级），读取功耗低至0.1 pJ/bit（比DRAM低约10倍）。采用混合键合后，由于缩短了互联长度（<10 μm），读取能效进一步优化20%。与同尺寸的Si FeFET相比，IGZO FeFET在保持相同读取速度时，写入电压可降低至2-3 V（Si需3-5 V），这得益于IGZO的宽禁带（约3.2 eV）和低漏电特性。在可靠性方面，经过10^11次读取周期后，阈值电压漂移小于50 mV，满足AI推理场景需求。

**技术影响与应用场景**：该工作为三维异构AI存储器提供了新的设计范式。在应用层面：1）边缘AI推理芯片：通过在逻辑芯片的BEOL层集成IGZO FeFET作为片上缓存，可大幅减少对外部DRAM的访问，降低系统功耗；2）高性能计算中的近存储处理：利用混合键合将NOR型FeFET芯粒堆叠在GPU/TPU旁，实现高带宽（理论可达TB/s级）的数据读取；3）神经形态计算：NOR阵列可模拟突触权重，无需频繁写入，契合在线学习中的稀疏更新模式。但该技术仍需解决阵列密度（目前约10-20 Mb/mm²）和写入耐久性（约10^6次）等挑战。总体而言，以读取为中心的DTCO方法为IGZO FeFET在三维异构系统中的实用化铺平了道路，尤其适用于对延迟和能效敏感的AI推理场景。

items.title: This is your laptop… on AI
items.description:
We're now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything. Nvidia's Jensen Huang made that clearer than anyone this week, when he described a completely new way of using our laptops - and a completely new kind of laptop made to support it. It's all very interesting, but it raises the same question we have around so many AI products: Does anyone actually want this?
On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David run through a lot of the products coming out of Microsoft Build and Google I/O, from Gemini S …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: We’re now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything. Nvidia’s Jensen Huang made that clearer than anyone this week, when he described a completely new way of using our laptops — and a completely new kind of laptop made to support it. It’s all very interesting, but it raises the same question we have around so many AI products: Does anyone actually want this?On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David run through a lot of the products coming out of Microsoft Build and Google I/O, from Gemini Spark to the Nvidia RTX Spark to Microsoft’s Scout and Solara projects. AI agents are everywhere, doing everything, and we’re not exactly sure how to feel about it. Are we due for a complete re-think of our laptops, just so they can run AI models? Or is “more powerful laptop” enough to get the job done?After that, it’s time for the Hype Desk, Brendan Carr is a Dummy, our thoughts about WWDC, and a deeply silly Meta hack. Watch | Listen | Get ad-freeAlso: We’re now a week in to The Vergecast’s new life as a daily podcast! We’ve talked about the state of posting, Nvidia’s chip ambitions, the Steroid Olympics, and Microsoft Build. We already have new ideas for the show and some stuff we want to improve, but we also want to hear how you’re feeling about the new format. Tell us everything! Call the Vergecast Hotline at 866-VERGE11, send us an email at vergecast@theverge.com, and tell us everything that’s on your mind. And make sure you subscribe so you don’t miss an episode!
items.author: David Pierce
items.titleZh: 这是你的笔记本电脑……在AI里
items.descriptionZh:
目前正值开发者大会密集召开的季节，一个贯穿始终的主题是：大型科技公司坚信AI将彻底改变我们做一切事情的方式。英伟达的黄仁勋在本周清晰地阐述了这一点，他描述了一种全新的笔记本电脑使用方式——以及一种为支持这种使用方式而设计的全新笔记本电脑。这一切听起来非常有趣，但同样也引出了我们在众多AI产品面前反复纠结的问题：真的有人需要这个吗？

在本期The Vergecast节目中，Nilay和David盘点了微软Build和Google I/O大会上发布的大量产品，从Gemini Spark、英伟达RTX Spark，到微软的Scout和Solara项目。AI智能体无处不在，包揽一切事务，而我们对这种局面感受复杂。我们是否真的需要为了运行AI模型而对笔记本电脑进行彻底的重新设计？还是说“更强大的笔记本电脑”就足以胜任了？

节目后半段进入了“炒作台”环节，讨论了“Brendan Carr是个傻瓜”的话题、对苹果WWDC的展望，以及一个极其愚蠢的Meta黑客事件。此外，The Vergecast已经转型为日更播客一周了！我们已经聊过社交网络的现状、英伟达的芯片野心、类固醇奥运会以及微软Build。我们对节目有了新想法，也有想要改进的地方，但更想听听你对新形式的感受。请告诉我们一切！拨打Vergecast热线866-VERGE11，或发送邮件至vergecast@theverge.com，分享你的想法。别忘了订阅，以免错过任何一期节目。

items.title: Intel will reportedly upgrade its Wildcat Lake refresh to an 8-core config next year, leak claims — top-end silicon tipped to feature 4 P-cores and 4 LP-E cores as part of 'Core 400' series
items.description: Intel's Wildcat Lake refresh that's supposedly debuting next year will shift focus to a more upmarket audience, only refreshing its Core 5 and Core 7 tiers. The new silicon at the top-end would feature 8 cores, up from 6 cores on Wildcat Lake right now, with the 4 P-cores and 4 LP-E cores. The Core 3 parts are claimed to remain unchanged.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel just launched its Core 300 series of mobile CPUs, also known as Wildcat Lake, for the budget segment a couple of months ago. But rumors for a next-gen refresh are already surfacing, with the latest one claiming that it will be a noticeable upgrade. Reliable tipster Jaykihn has just suggested that the Wildcat Lake refresh due next year will move to an 8-core config, up from the 6-cores that Wildcat Lake has right now.Intel "Wildcat Lake" refresh for 2027 ⤵️ https://t.co/qucfyAkxhmJune 5, 2026The post you can expand above says "4+0+4," in reference to a rumored Wildcat Lake Refresh chip with 4 P-cores, 0 E-cores, and 4 LP-E cores, totaling out to 8 cores. Wildcat Lake today also has 4 LP-E cores but only 2 P-cores, so we're looking at a doubling in this aspect, and that should translate to real-world performance improvements. Keep in mind that these are Cougar Cove P-cores along with Darkmont-based LP-E cores, borrowed from Panther Lake silicon.After all, Wildcat Lake is essentially just a cost-efficient and downsized version of Panther Lake that has an infused, monolithic CPU+GPU tile instead of separate chiplets bonded together — both are manufactured using Intel's 18A process. Wildcat Lake is, therefore, branded as the "Core 300" series, with the "Ultra" moniker removed to specify its lower-end capabilities.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Just two days ago, a leak from the same insider claimed that a 6-core Nova Lake mobile part was cancelled because that slot would be better filled by the Wildcat Lake refresh instead. Intel likely didn't want the two to overlap, and now that we know about a potential 8-core config, it makes even more sense. Jaykihn also replied, saying that Wildcat Lake refresh will still have 2 Xe3 cores for integrated graphics.In the replies to the post attached above, Videocardz separately claims the Wildcat Lake refresh would be part of the Core 400 series, but only for the Core 5 and Core 7 SKUs. The lowest-end Core 3 SKUs would still be branded as the Core 300 series, as they will not be getting refreshed. This could mean Intel is trying to target a more upscale audience with its Wildcat Lake refresh, while the cheapest parts remain unchanged. items.titleZh: 据称英特尔明年将升级Wildcat Lake刷新版至8核配置——顶级芯片将配备4个P核和4个LP-E核，属于'Core 400'系列
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔近日发布了面向入门级市场的Core 300系列移动处理器（代号Wildcat Lake），但关于其下一代刷新版本的传闻已开始浮现。据可靠爆料人Jaykihn透露，计划于2027年（注：原文发布日期为2026年6月，实际时间线存在矛盾，可能是笔误或预测）推出的Wildcat Lake Refresh将迎来显著升级，核心配置从当前的6核心（2个性能核+4个低功耗能效核）提升至8核心（4个性能核+0个能效核+4个低功耗能效核）。这意味着性能核数量翻倍，有望带来实质性的多线程与单线程性能提升。

核心技术方面，Wildcat Lake Refresh采用与Panther Lake相同的Cougar Cove架构性能核（P-core）和Darkmont架构低功耗能效核（LP-E core），两者均基于英特尔18A制程工艺。值得注意的是，Wildcat Lake本质上是Panther Lake的成本优化缩小版本，采用一体化CPU+GPU单片式设计，而非分离式芯片互连方案。这也正是它被归入“Core 300”系列、并去掉“Ultra”标识以表明其入门级定位的原因。

性能参数与配置对比：当前Wildcat Lake仅有2个P-core和4个LP-E core，而Refresh版本将P-core增加到4个，LP-E core保持4个，总计8核心。虽然取消了一组传统E-core，但低功耗LP-E核心在能效和后台任务处理上仍能提供支持，整体计算能力将明显增强。此外，集成显卡方面仍保留2个Xe3核心，并未缩水。

产品命名与市场定位上，另一家媒体VideoCardz补充称，Wildcat Lake Refresh将归属于Core 400系列，仅覆盖Core 5和Core 7两个SKU层级；而最低端的Core 3 SKU仍沿用Core 300系列且不获刷新。这暗示英特尔试图通过Refresh版本瞄准更高价位市场，入门级则维持现有产品不变。此前也有消息称，一颗原本规划的6核心Nova Lake移动版已被取消，原因正是英特尔认为Wildcat Lake Refresh更适合填补该定位，避免产品重叠。

综合来看，Wildcat Lake Refresh的8核配置、18A工艺以及成熟的架构继承，使其在入门到中端移动市场具备较强的竞争力，尤其适用于轻薄本、教育本和低成本高性能需求场景。其性能提升将直接影响多任务处理、轻量级创作和日常办公体验，而保留的Xe3核显也为轻度图形和视频解码提供了保障。随着发布临近，更多规格细节将逐步浮出水面。

items.title: Seattle to pass one-year AI data center moratorium next week — will use window to study community impact of AI buildouts
items.description: Two Seattle city council committees have passed a one-year moratorium and a resolution on data centers. The measures are still up for a vote in the full council, but many consider that simply as a formality.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Two city council committees just passed a data center moratorium in Seattle, making it the biggest jurisdiction in the U.S. to enforce a ban on these projects. According to The Guardian, the moratorium and an accompanying resolution must still go through a full council vote on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, but this is just a formality given the massive support that the data center ban has from the general public.Several other towns, cities, counties, and areas in the United States have passed data center bans, especially as projects like these have increasingly become unpopular among Americans. Still, many didn’t expect tech-industry heavy Seattle to follow them in their footsteps.Seattle mayor Katie Wilson first heard about the plan to build five large data centers near the city in April, after the Seattle Times broke the story. “That was the first that I, as the mayor, had heard about this,” Wilson said. “Both I and many of the councilmembers were happy to move toward a moratorium, especially knowing that there was really strong public support out there for that course of action.”Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The city intends to spend the one-year hold on data center development to study its impact on the community surrounding it and what the local government should do to ensure that its constituents benefit from the project. This includes putting up pollution standards, determining energy connection requirements, setting contract terms, setting labor standards, and other regulations that will apply to data centers. Wilson also mentioned that Seattle will ascertain if these projects are a “good use of urban land” during this period.Seattle’s residents supported the temporary moratorium, with more than 50 residents showing their support for it when the city hall opened public comments on the measure. Aside from increasing electricity bills and impacts on the water supply, some of the concerns that were raised at this time included the climate impact of data centers, especially behind-the-meter projects that are often powered by jet engines running on fossil fuels, noise pollution, and the conversion of arable land into sprawling campuses. You may like Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects The city council’s decision to pass the one-year ban is in stark contrast to the actions of some politicians in other states that support data center developments despite opposition. For example, one Indiana mayor was caught on camera insulting his constituents who were against a proposed data center project in their area, while a Utah senator slapped the phone out of the hand of a reporter who was covering a story about the cases of harassment against his business because of his support for a 40,000-acre site in Box Elder County. items.titleZh: 西雅图下周将通过对AI数据中心的一年禁令——利用窗口期研究AI建设对社区的影响
items.descriptionZh:
西雅图市两个市议会委员会近日通过了一项数据中心建设暂停令（moratorium），使该市成为美国实施此类项目禁令的最大司法管辖区。据《卫报》报道，该暂停令及配套决议仍需在2026年6月9日（星期二）提交全体市议会投票，但鉴于公众对该禁令的广泛支持，这几乎只是走个形式。

此前，美国已有多个城镇、县及地区通过了数据中心禁令，尤其是随着此类项目在美国民众中的受欢迎程度持续下降。但许多人并未预料到，作为科技产业重镇的西雅图也会加入这一行列。西雅图市长凯蒂·威尔逊表示，她是在今年4月《西雅图时报》率先披露后，才首次听说该市附近计划建设五个大型数据中心的消息。“作为市长，我也是第一次听说，”威尔逊说，“我和许多市议会议员都很乐意推进暂停令，尤其是知道公众对此有非常强烈的支持。”

该市计划利用这一年的数据中心开发暂停期，研究项目对周边社区的影响，以及地方政府应如何确保其选民从项目中受益。研究内容包括制定污染标准、确定能源连接要求、设定合同条款、制定劳工标准以及适用于数据中心的其他法规。威尔逊还提到，在此期间西雅图将评估这些项目是否为“城市土地的合理利用”。

西雅图居民支持这一临时暂停令。在市政厅就该措施开放公众意见征询时，超过50名居民表达了支持。除了电费上涨和对供水的影响外，市民们还提出了其他担忧，包括数据中心的气候影响（特别是那些通常由化石燃料喷气发动机供电的表后项目）、噪音污染，以及将可耕地转变为庞大的数据中心园区。

西雅图市议会的这一决定，与一些其他州政界人士无视反对、支持数据中心发展的做法形成鲜明对比。例如，印第安纳州一位市长被拍到辱骂反对当地数据中心项目的选民；犹他州一位参议员则因支持在博克斯埃尔德县开发一个4万英亩的数据中心园区，在记者报道其企业遭受骚扰案件时，直接打掉了记者手中的手机。

items.title: Nvidia is reportedly still planning fabled RTX 50 Super series for 2026, leak claims — lineup could now include a potential 'RTX 5060 Super' with 12GB of VRAM
items.description: For almost a year, the RTX 50 Super series has been part of the rumor mill, but with the AI boom snatching production lines, causing memory prices to skyrocket, hype for the lineup had died down. Now, a potential RTX 5060 Super with 12GB of VRAM is apparently in the works, with the 50 Super series as a whole allegedly getting "back on track."
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A potential "Super" refresh for Nvidia's Blackwell 50-series GPUs has been part of the news cycle for almost a year at this point, with the last substantial sighting coming from Seasonic's PSU calculator nine months ago. Since then, the rumor mill has been mostly silent due to an AI boom-sized component crisis that engulfed all production lines. But now, it seems like the RTX 50 Super series is "back on track," with leaker MEGAsizeGPU claiming that there's even a new SKU in the works.RTX 50 Super is back on track. This time includes 5060 12G ( or maybe it will have a new name as 5060 super )June 5, 2026We've already heard the RTX 5070 Super, RTX 5070 Ti Super, and RTX 5080 Super mentioned before, but this time, there's also an RTX 5060 Super in the mix. This rumored GPU could come with 12GB of GDDR7 RAM, according to the leak, possibly using 4x 3GB modules saturated across the same 128-bit bus that the regular RTX 5060 has. This card could also just be called RTX 5060 12GB, as Nvidia has named a few SKUs like that previously.The leak doesn't mention other GPUs specifically, but through prior rumors, we can infer that the RTX 5070 Super might feature 18GB of VRAM, while both the RTX 5070 Ti Super and the RTX 5080 Super are suggested to rock 24GB pools — all enabled by 3GB GDDR7 chips. One of the replies in the post above claims that specs for these three SKUs apparently remain unchanged.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Swipe to scroll horizontallyRumored * RTX 50 Super detailsGraphics CardRTX 5080 Super*RTX 5080RTX 5070 Ti Super*RTX 5070 TiRTX 5070 Super*RTX 5070 RTX 5060 12GB (Super)*RTX 5060 ArchitectureGB203GB203GB203 GB203 GB205GB205GB206GB206VRAM (GDDR7)24GB16GB24GB16GB18GB12GB12GB8GBVRAM Bus Width256-bit256-bit256-bit256-bit192-bit192-bit128-bit128-bitCUDA Cores10,75210,7528,9608,9606,4006,144?3,840TGP415W360W350W300W275W250W?145W*= unconfirmed models based on leaks and rumorsWe don't know when these GPUs will actually be released, but the leaker says he expects them to still launch in 2026. With DRAM being as expensive as it is right now, and GPU prices affected by the AI boom, these new RTX 50 Super SKUs may not exactly be good value. Back in November of last year, there were murmurs of these GPUs even being cancelled due to just how valuable 28Gbps GDDR7 modules had become. You may like Rumored RTX 5050 9GB could make hay from recycled RTX 5060 silicon Nvidia RTX 3060 comeback in 2026 could alleviate soaring GPU prices and memory shortages Nvidia quietly launches 12GB RTX 5070 laptop GPU To add fuel to the fire, Nvidia itself quashed rumors of an RTX 50 Super series announcement at CES 2026 earlier this year. Add to that the leaked RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM, highlighting the sheer desperation of the moment, and any hope left was killed. An overpriced RTX 50 Super series launching in these circumstances was unlikely. So, a single churn of the rumor mill isn't enough to reignite excitement; we'll have to wait and see if the frequency of these leaks once again picks up. items.titleZh: 据报道，英伟达仍在计划2026年推出传说中的RTX 50 Super系列——泄露称，阵容可能包括潜在拥有12GB显存的“RTX 5060 Super”
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达RTX 50 Super系列显卡可能重获新生：泄漏显示5060 Super将配备12GB GDDR7显存，但定价与发布时间仍存变数

在经历了近一年的沉寂后，关于英伟达Blackwell架构RTX 50系列“Super”升级版的传闻再次浮出水面。此前，由于AI热潮引发的GPU供应链危机和显存组件短缺，这一系列曾被普遍认为已遭取消。但爆料者MEGAsizeGPU近日声称，RTX 50 Super系列“重回正轨”，甚至新增了一个此前未提及的SKU——RTX 5060 Super（或命名为RTX 5060 12GB）。

核心技术与创新点：3GB GDDR7芯片带来的显存升级

这次Super更新的核心卖点在于显存容量的显著提升，而技术基础是英伟达采用新一代3GB GDDR7显存芯片。传统上，GDDR7芯片多为2GB/颗，3GB芯片允许在同样的显存位宽下实现1.5倍的容量增长。

具体而言，泄漏信息显示RTX 5060 Super（或RTX 5060 12GB）将配备12GB GDDR7显存，但显存位宽依然是128-bit。这意味着它使用了4颗3GB GDDR7芯片，而非标准RTX 5060的8GB（4颗2GB芯片）。这一做法与英伟达此前的命名策略类似（例如曾推出“RTX 3060 12GB”而非“3060 Super”）。

对于更高端型号，泄漏的规格表（基于此前流传的传闻）勾勒出如下配置：
- **RTX 5080 Super**: 24GB GDDR7，256-bit位宽，CUDA核心数10,752，TGP 415W（对比标准RTX 5080的16GB/360W）
- **RTX 5070 Ti Super**: 24GB GDDR7，256-bit位宽，CUDA核心数8,960，TGP 350W（对比标准RTX 5070 Ti的16GB/300W）
- **RTX 5070 Super**: 18GB GDDR7，192-bit位宽，CUDA核心数6,400，TGP 275W（对比标准RTX 5070的12GB/250W）
- **RTX 5060 Super (12GB)**: 12GB GDDR7，128-bit位宽，CUDA核心数3,840，TGP约145W（对比标准RTX 5060的8GB/未知）

注意，这些规格均未得到英伟达官方确认，且CUDA核心数的变化（例如5070 Super从标准的6,144提升至6,400）暗示可能使用了不同的GPU芯片（GB205 vs GB206），或是在同芯片上启用了更多单元。

性能参数与市场背景：AI泡沫下的昂贵升级

尽管显存容量增加对游戏和多任务处理是利好，但当前的市场环境可能导致这些Super SKU的性价比并不理想。新闻指出，“DRAM价格目前非常昂贵”，且28Gbps GDDR7芯片因AI需求变得极为珍贵。早在2024年11月，就曾有传言称这些Super显卡可能被取消——因为显存芯片本身的价值已经高到让“升级版”缺乏商业合理性。

此外，英伟达本身在2026年初的CES上明确否认了RTX 50 Super系列发布的传闻，而同期泄漏的“RTX 5050 9GB”更被解读为显存短缺下的妥协方案。因此，本次“回归”传闻的可信度仍有待观察。

技术影响与应用场景：AI工作负载的福音，但时机尴尬

如果这些Super显卡最终面世，对特定用户群将产生显著影响：
1. **游戏玩家**：12GB/18GB/24GB的显存使得在4K分辨率下开启高材质包和光追成为可能，尤其适合《赛博朋克2077》等显存敏感型游戏。RTX 5080 Super的24GB显存也使其更接近专业卡的规格，能满足大型模组或高分辨率纹理需求。
2. **AI/内容创作者**：更大的显存意味着可以本地运行更大参数的AI模型（如Llama 3.1 8B的量化版本），或者进行更高分辨率的图像/视频生成。RTX 5080 Super的24GB甚至可部分替代RTX 6000 Ada等专业卡。
3. **价格与供需**：然而，在当下显卡价格被AI需求推高的时期，这些“升级版”的定价很可能远高于标准版。如果RTX 5080 Super的售价显著高于RTX 5080（而后者本身已因缺货而溢价），其市场接受度存疑。

结论：仍需等待更多证据

目前，唯一来自爆料者的明确时间是“预计2026年内发布”。但考虑到英伟达官方态度暧昧、供应链成本高企，以及此前谣言反复，这次泄漏可能只是又一次“炒冷饭”。只有在更多独立消息源（如后续的基准测试泄露或供应链报告）出现后，才能判断RTX 50 Super系列是否真的能重见天日。对于关注高端显卡的用户，建议保持观望，同时关注DRAM价格走势——若显存成本在2026年下半年回落，英伟达推出Super系列的经济动力才会增强。

items.title: Industry coalition urges Trump administration to take urgent action as AI data centers' extreme memory consumption threatens other industries — AI-driven memory chip shortage could raise prices in automotive, medical, telecommunications sectors
items.description: A coalition of nine U.S. trade associations has urged the Trump administration to address an AI-driven memory chip shortage, warning that soaring DRAM prices and constrained supply could raise costs for consumer electronics, automobiles, medical devices, and broadband infrastructure while disrupting supply chains through at least 2027.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A coalition of nine US trade associations has urged the Trump administration to take immediate action on what it describes as an emerging memory chip shortage driven by the explosive growth of AI data centers. In a June 3 letter sent to US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent shared with Tom's Hardware, the organizations — representing telecommunications providers, automakers, medical device manufacturers, and major retailers — warned that AI infrastructure deployments are consuming an outsized share of global memory production, creating supply constraints and price increases that could ripple across large segments of the US economy.The coalition warned that the AI data center expansion, which has consumed an unprecedented share of global memory capacity, has led to a memory chip shortage that could lead to higher prices for consumer electronics, increased costs for broadband and telecommunications infrastructure, disruptions to automobile and medical device production, and delays affecting federal contractors attempting to fulfill government procurement obligations. The letter argues that these risks are emerging despite billions of dollars of US investment intended to strengthen domestic semiconductor supply chains.The signatories acknowledged AI's importance but argue it shouldn't come at the expense of the rest of the economy. "While recent developments in AI offer the promise of generational technological advances and are important for US tech leadership, we must also ensure other key industries are not negatively impacted by this disruption in the marketplace," the coalition said.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The organizations are asking the administration to work directly with memory suppliers and major chip buyers to address the imbalance. Their recommendations include accelerating expansion of memory manufacturing capacity in the United States and allied nations, using trade agreements to strengthen supply-chain resilience, ensuring adequate memory supply for non-AI industries, leveraging CHIPS Act programs where possible, and reducing regulatory barriers that may slow capacity growth."We urge the Administration to work with memory chipmakers and chip buyers to assess steps that can be taken to address this imbalance in the memory market and protect against harm to consumers, workers, and businesses of all sizes," the letter states. You may like Samsung and SK hynix warn AI-driven memory shortages could last until 2027 and beyond, as HBM demand explodes Chinese chip industry leaders admit the country lags five to ten years behind in AI data center chips US trade deficit hits a record $1.2 trillion as AI hardware imports surge under the Trump administration The warning arrives as memory manufacturers increasingly prioritize high-bandwidth memory (HBM), the specialized memory used in AI accelerators from companies such as Nvidia and AMD. Demand for HBM has surged over the past two years as hyperscalers race to deploy larger AI clusters, prompting memory suppliers to devote an increasing share of their production capacity to AI-oriented products.Samsung and SK Hynix — which together with Micron control over 95% of global DRAM production — have been diverting wafer capacity toward high-margin HBM for AI accelerators, starving the commodity DRAM and NAND markets in the process. Both companies warned in April that significant shortages will continue through at least 2027. IDC, meanwhile, has already revised its 2026 PC market forecast downward by up to 9% as a direct consequence of memory scarcity and rising prices.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIndustry analysts have repeatedly warned for months that AI demand is reshaping the economics of the memory market. While memory shortages have historically been cyclical, the coalition argues that AI infrastructure spending is creating a structural shift large enough to affect industries far removed from data centers. The letter marks the first coordinated, multi-industry push for federal intervention. Whether the administration will respond — and how — remains to be seen. items.titleZh: 行业联盟敦促特朗普政府采取紧急行动，因AI数据中心极端内存消耗威胁其他行业——AI驱动的内存芯片短缺可能导致汽车、医疗、电信行业价格上涨
items.descriptionZh:
美国九个主要贸易协会联合致信特朗普政府，警告由人工智能（AI）数据中心狂飙式扩张引发的内存芯片短缺正在威胁美国经济多个关键行业。这份6月3日致美国商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克和财政部长斯科特·贝森特的信函（已分享给Tom's Hardware）由电信运营商、汽车制造商、医疗设备厂商及大型零售商代表联名签署，指出AI基础设施部署正在吞噬全球内存生产的超比例份额，导致供应紧张和价格上涨，其影响可能波及美国经济的广泛领域。

信函指出，AI数据中心的扩张已消耗了前所未有的全球内存产能，导致内存芯片短缺，进而可能推高消费电子产品价格，增加宽带和电信基础设施成本，扰乱汽车和医疗设备生产，甚至影响联邦承包商履行政府采购义务。尽管美国已投入数十亿美元加强国内半导体供应链，这些风险仍在加剧。签署方承认AI的重要性，但强调不应以牺牲其他经济部门为代价：“AI发展带来代际技术进步，对美国技术领导力至关重要，但我们必须确保其他关键行业不因市场扰乱而遭受负面冲击。”

该联盟要求政府直接与内存供应商和主要芯片买家合作解决供需失衡，具体建议包括：加速美国及盟国的内存制造产能扩张；利用贸易协定增强供应链韧性；确保非AI行业获得充足内存供应；尽可能运用《芯片与科学法案》项目；以及减少阻碍产能增长的监管壁垒。信函称：“我们敦促政府与内存芯片制造商和买家合作，评估措施以解决内存市场失衡，保护消费者、工人和各类企业免受损害。”

这一警告正值内存制造商日益优先生产高带宽内存（HBM）——用于英伟达和AMD等公司AI加速器的专用内存。过去两年，随着超大规模云服务商竞相部署更大规模AI集群，HBM需求激增，促使内存供应商将越来越大的产能转向AI产品。三星、SK海力士以及美光（三者合计控制全球超过95%的DRAM生产）正在将晶圆产能转向高利润的AI用HBM，导致普通DRAM和NAND市场“缺粮”。这两家公司已在4月警告，显著短缺将持续至少到2027年。国际数据公司（IDC）因此将2026年PC市场预测下调了最多9%，直接归因于内存稀缺和价格上涨。

行业分析师数月来反复警告，AI需求正在重塑内存市场的经济规律。虽然内存短缺历史上具有周期性，但该联盟认为，AI基础设施支出正在造成结构性转变，其影响范围远超数据中心。这封信标志着首次有跨行业联盟协调推动联邦干预。政府是否会回应以及如何回应，尚待观察。

items.title: Jensen Huang says 'every edge device will become autonomous' — Nvidia maps one computing pattern from the cloud to robotics
items.description: "There's a new computing pattern," the Nvidia CEO told reporters at a press gaggle the day after his GTC Taipei keynote.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter When not being spotted at night markets or meeting crowds of adoring fans, the hardware industry’s biggest celebrity, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, spent most of his time at Computex this week making the case that computing as we know it is collapsing into one repeatable pattern built for AI agents; a blueprint that now runs across the cloud, the PC, the car, and the robot."There's a new computing pattern," the Nvidia CEO told reporters at a press gaggle the day after his GTC Taipei keynote, describing an agent architecture he calls a harness that orchestrates reasoning, memory, and tool use the same way whether it sits in a data center or a laptop.He tied that claim to every product Nvidia detailed at the show, from the Vera data-center CPU now in full production to RTX Spark, its first Windows PC platform, shipping in laptops this fall.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: One pattern, every machineHuang told the room that he repeats the same keynote structure on purpose. "Every time I give you a keynote, it's like Top Gun 17, and it's exactly the same architecture," he said, "because I want you to know that the future of computing is this." The pattern begins with training and inference in the cloud and pushes outward to everything else: "Every edge device will become autonomous. Every edge device will have agentic systems."He ran that blueprint through self-driving cars, humanoid robots, Nokia base stations, and imaging satellites, casting each as the same agent profile on different hardware. Curiously, the self-driving car got quite a bit of airtime, with Huang describing Nvidia's Alpamayo driving stack as a system that reasons in language rather than reacting to images, one that could read a "skill file" and watch a tutorial video to operate unfamiliar machinery the way a person would. "That's how autonomous vehicles are going to work in the future," he said. "It's essentially that agentic computing pattern with a physical AI model." You may like Jensen Huang says Nvidia wants to 'reinvent the single most important tool of humanity' with RTX Spark Nvidia unveils RTX Spark Superchip for laptops and desktop PCs at Computex 2026 Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote live blog — Vera Rubin GPUs and CPUs, DLSS 5, and the 'future of technology' (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)A CPU that generates tokens, not coresVera, on the data center side, is an 88-core Arm processor that Nvidia is now in full production with, pitching it as a chip built for agents rather than human users. "We built Vera for agents to use," Huang said. "Until six months ago, there were no agents, so that's the definition of a $0 billion market."A hyperscale CPU piles on cores because humans lease them by the hundred, where an agent, Huang argued, "doesn't want to rent the CPU core, the agent wants to generate tokens." That pushed Nvidia toward single-thread speed and memory bandwidth over core count, and Huang claimed Vera offers the largest step up in single-threaded performance he has seen "in 25 years." His reasoning ties back to latency: "Humans are more patient than agents. Agents, they're working at nanosecond scale, not second scale."Nvidia claims 1.8 times faster task completion than x86 and a 1.5 times instructions-per-clock gain over its Grace predecessor, with a 256-chip liquid-cooled Vera rack it says reaches six times the throughput of a conventional CPU rack. The chip ships on the back of nearly 2.5 million Grace units sold, and Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI, ByteDance, CoreWeave, and Oracle are named as early customers. CFO Colette Kress told investors on Nvidia's latest earnings call that the company sees "nearly $20 billion in total CPU revenue this year".Phoronix's first public Vera benchmarks in May measured it roughly 10% ahead of AMD's 64-core EPYC 9575F and about 55% ahead of Intel's 128-core Xeon 6980P across selected Linux workloads. Nvidia ran those tests on pre-production silicon at its own headquarters, limited them to workloads it considers relevant, and, by Phoronix's account, switched off CPU power and frequency monitoring for the session. (Image credit: Nvidia)Reinventing the PC after 40 yearsAs for RTX Spark, Huang says that it’s the first real rethink of the PC in four decades. "We have an opportunity after 40 years to go reinvent it for the age of AI," he said, predicting the machine shifts "from your PC being a tool to now really your PC being your system." He pushed even further: "Your laptop is going to be your R2-D2." What to read next Nvidia's RTX Spark could caplitalize where Qualcomm's Arm-based efforts have not Nvidia lays out RTX Spark roadmap for laptops and desktop PCs at Computex 2026 Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says, "I love constraints," praising the ongoing shortages caused by AI The top RTX Spark part, internally N1X, pairs a 20-core Arm CPU built by MediaTek (10 Cortex-X925 performance cores and 10 Cortex-A725 efficiency cores) with a Blackwell GPU carrying 6,144 CUDA cores, up to 128GB of LPDDR5X unified memory, and a 600 GB/s NVLink-C2C link, all on TSMC's 3nm node. Huang justified these specs with the same impatience he applied to Vera, arguing that an agent driving the machine won’t wait, so the software it touches, from Adobe to Blender, "cannot be slow."The platform is launching in a market that Qualcomm had effectively dominated until its Windows on Arm exclusivity with Microsoft lapsed. Fall 2026 laptops are confirmed from Microsoft, Dell, HP, ASUS, Lenovo, and MSI, with Acer and Gigabyte to follow, and Nvidia says anti-cheat engines, including Easy Anti-Cheat and Denuvo, run natively on the chip. Asked why Nvidia would enter a low-margin business it has steered clear of for years, Huang said, "We don't really have to choose. The real question is, can we make a contribution?"Vera's 88 cores are Nvidia's own custom Olympus design, its first ground-up server core since the Denver and Carmel projects, while RTX Spark's 20 cores are Arm's off-the-shelf Cortex reference designs licensed through MediaTek, one of them already a generation old. Huang's "same pattern everywhere" runs, at the silicon level, on two different CPUs.When asked whether the Olympus cores would come to Windows PCs, Huang declined to commit. "Our preference is to use off-the-shelf cores whenever we can, because Arm also builds good cores," he said, adding that Olympus was pushed toward single-thread speed in a way standard many-core Arm parts weren’t: "We wanted to push single-threaded performance as far as we could push it." The first PC chip using Nvidia's own cores isn’t expected until 2028. Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley estimates Vera at around $5,000 per socket inside a vertically integrated rack.What about memory?DRAM contract prices have climbed sharply through 2026 as makers divert wafers to high-bandwidth memory, and Nvidia remains short of supply even as it locks in capacity, by Huang's own account: "We have enough supply for very robust growth. However, we are supply constrained.""One of the best ways to improve memory use is to use extremely, extremely low precision," Huang said, pointing to NVFP4, Nvidia's 4-bit floating-point format that scales between four, eight, 16, and 32 bits and roughly doubles the parameters that fit in a given memory pool, the trick that lets RTX Spark hold larger models in its 128GB. He paired it with neural texture compression that cuts game texture memory by up to eight times in Nvidia's demos. At SK hynix's booth during the show, Huang signed an HBM4E wafer with the words "Please Make More."
items.author: Luke James
items.titleZh: 黄仁勋称“每个边缘设备都将实现自主”——英伟达将一种计算模式从云端映射到机器人领域
items.descriptionZh:
2026年台北国际电脑展（Computex）期间，英伟达（Nvidia）CEO黄仁勋（Jensen Huang）花了大量时间向业界阐述一个核心观点：传统的计算模式正在瓦解，取而代之的是一种专为AI Agent（智能体）构建的可重复计算范式。这一蓝图从云端延伸到PC、汽车和机器人，所有设备都将遵循同一套架构逻辑。“我们正在见证一种新的计算模式，”黄仁勋在主题演讲后的记者会上表示。他描述了一种被他称为“harness”（束缚装置）的Agent架构，能够统一协调推理、记忆和工具调用，无论它运行在数据中心还是笔记本电脑上。他还将这一论断与英伟达在展会上发布的所有产品挂钩，从现已全面投产的Vera数据中心CPU，到今秋将随笔记本电脑出货的首个Windows PC平台RTX Spark。

**统一计算模式：从云端到边缘的全自主化**

黄仁勋坦言，他每次主题演讲都刻意重复相同的架构叙事：“就像《壮志凌云17》一样，每次都是同样的架构——因为我想让你们知道，计算的未来就是如此。”这一模式始于云端的训练和推理，然后向外延伸至所有边缘设备。“每一个边缘设备都将变得自主，每一个边缘设备都将拥有Agent系统。”他将这一蓝图应用到自动驾驶汽车、人形机器人、诺基亚基站和成像卫星上，将每一项都描述为运行在不同硬件上的同一类Agent。特别值得一提的是，自动驾驶汽车占据了相当多的篇幅。黄仁勋介绍英伟达的Alpamayo驾驶栈时，将其描述为一种用语言推理而非被动响应图像的系统——它能读取“技能文件”，像人一样观看教学视频来操作陌生设备。“这就是未来自动驾驶汽车的工作方式，本质上就是带有物理AI模型的Agent计算模式。”

**Vera CPU：为Agent生成Token而非为人类提供核心**

在数据中心侧，Vera是一款88核Arm处理器，现已全面投产。黄仁勋将其定位为“为Agent而非人类用户构建的芯片”。他解释道：“我们为Agent打造了Vera。而直到六个月前，市场上还没有Agent，所以这定义了一个零美元的市场。”传统超大规模CPU堆叠大量核心，是因为人类用户按百核数量租用；而Agent“不想租用CPU核心，它想生成Token”。这一理念促使英伟达将重点放在单线程速度和内存带宽上，而非核心数量。黄仁勋声称，Vera提供了他“25年来见过的最大的单线程性能提升”。理由与延迟有关：“人类的耐心比Agent强。Agent的工作尺度是纳秒级，而非秒级。”

英伟达宣称，Vera的任务完成速度是x86的1.8倍，每周期指令数（IPC）较其前代Grace提升1.5倍。一个由256颗Vera芯片组成的液冷机架，其吞吐量达到传统CPU机架的6倍。该芯片基于已售出的近250万颗Grace单元，早期客户包括Anthropic、OpenAI、xAI、字节跳动、CoreWeave和Oracle。英伟达CFO Colette Kress在最新财报电话会上向投资者表示，公司预计今年CPU总收入接近200亿美元。Phoronix在5月发布的Vera早期基准测试显示，在选定的Linux工作负载中，Vera比AMD的64核EPYC 9575F大约快10%，比Intel的128核Xeon 6980P大约快55%。不过，这些测试是在英伟达总部的预生产硅片上运行的，仅限于英伟达认为相关的负载，且根据Phoronix的记录，测试期间CPU功耗和频率监控被关闭。

**RTX Spark：40年来首次重新发明PC**

对于RTX Spark，黄仁勋称之为40年来PC的第一次真正反思。“40年后，我们有机会为AI时代重新发明PC。”他预测，这台机器将从“作为工具的个人电脑”转变为“作为你的系统的个人电脑”。他甚至进一步比喻：“你的笔记本电脑将成为你的R2-D2。”顶级RTX Spark芯片（内部代号N1X）采用联发科制造的20核Arm CPU（10个Cortex-X925性能核心和10个Cortex-A725能效核心），搭配拥有6144个CUDA核心的Blackwell GPU，最高支持128GB LPDDR5X统一内存，通过600 GB/s的NVLink-C2C链路连接，全部基于台积电3nm制程。黄仁勋用与Vera同样的“不耐烦”逻辑来证明这些规格的合理性：驱动机器的Agent不会等待，因此它接触的软件（从Adobe到Blender）“不能慢”。

该平台进入的市场此前由高通凭借与微软的Windows on Arm独占协议主导，但随着该协议到期，局面正在改变。微软、戴尔、惠普、华硕、联想和微星已确认将在2026年秋季推出搭载RTX Spark的笔记本电脑，宏碁和技嘉紧随其后。英伟达表示，包括Easy Anti-Cheat和Denuvo在内的反作弊引擎已原生支持该芯片。当被问及为何英伟达要进入一个多年来一直回避的低利润业务时，黄仁勋回答：“我们不必非得做选择。真正的问题是，我们能做出什么贡献？”

**两种CPU内核策略**

值得注意的是，Vera的88个核心采用英伟达自研的定制Olympus设计——这是继Denver和Carmel项目之后，英伟达第一个完全自主的服务器核心。而RTX Spark的20个核心则采用了Arm的现成Cortex参考设计，并通过联发科授权，其中一些核心甚至已落后一代。黄仁勋“处处同一模式”的论点，在硅片层面实际上运行着两种不同的CPU。当被问及Olympus核心是否会出现在Windows PC上时，黄仁勋拒绝承诺。“我们倾向于尽可能使用现成的核心，因为Arm也制造了好的核心。”他补充说，Olympus在单线程速度上的极致追求，是标准多核Arm部件所不具备的。“我们想尽可能地把单线程性能推到极致。”预计首款使用英伟达自研核心的PC芯片要到2028年才能问世。与此同时，摩根士丹利估计，在垂直整合的机架中，Vera每颗插座的售价约为5000美元。

**内存困境与精度突破**

2026年DRAM合约价格大幅上涨，因为制造商将晶圆产能转向HBM（高带宽内存）。英伟达尽管已锁定产能，仍面临短缺。黄仁勋坦言：“我们有足够供应实现非常强劲的增长，但供应依然受限。”“改善内存使用的最佳方法之一是使用极其低的精度。”他指向NVFP4——英伟达的4位浮点格式，可在4、8、16、32位之间灵活缩放，使给定内存池能容纳的参数数量大约翻倍——这正是让RTX Spark用128GB内存运行更大模型的诀窍。他还搭配了神经纹理压缩技术，在英伟达的演示中可将游戏纹理内存占用降低多达8倍。在展会期间，黄仁勋到访SK海力士展台，在一块HBM4E晶圆上签下“请多生产”的字样。

items.title: Meta putting up tents across the US to house AI servers, like ‘a scene out of the movie Mad Max’ — structures take three months to build and use jet engines for power
items.description: Meta is reportedly building more tents that house expensive data centers across the U.S., as it reportedly cuts construction time from two to three years to just a few months. It's also bringing its own power instead of relying on electricity from the grid.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 12 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Meta has moved from building traditional structures for its data centers to putting up tents across the U.S. and sticking AI servers inside them. Michael Thomas, founder of market intelligence and data center tracking firm Cleanview Energy, said on X that the AI tech firm has already built or is in the process of constructing three data centers that use the strategy.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowOne site, located in New Albany, Ohio, already has five buildings that took approximately two to three years to complete. The company then started putting up five tents, with an area of around 125,000 square feet each, in the area. City permits seen by Cleanview Energy say that the construction started in April 2026, while recent satellite images show that the structures have already been completed.Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg first announced the strategy of pitching tents and filling them with AI servers last year. It seems that he wanted the infrastructure to come online quickly while demand for compute is increasing exponentially. It’s said that Meta is inspired by Elon Musk’s feat with xAI, which built a 100,000-strong AI data center in just 19 days in 2024 — something which usually takes four years, according to Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. The technique is apparently quite effective, and it’s now being applied to two other sites, including one in Tennessee.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Putting AI servers inside tents, officially called “rapid deployment structures,” is one of the more unique approaches to the AI build-out, Thomas said. They’re certainly not as sturdy as physical buildings made from steel and concrete, with one commenter comparing it to the “classic $10k racing bike with a $9 lock” situation. Nevertheless, the company has probably weighed the pros and cons of such a setup and has decided that it was worth taking the risk to gain an advantage in the AI infrastructure race.Another factor that allowed Meta to bring its data centers online at a much faster pace is its use of “behind-the-meter” power, in which the company installed its own turbines to produce power on-site rather than relying on grid power. This is similar to what Musk did with his Memphis Supercluster, which he initially powered with portable power generators. However, Meta’s turbines would be a permanent feature on the Ohio site, as it’s designed to run independently of the power grid. You may like Meta to fund seven new natural gas power plants to fuel AI data centers — Entergy partnership to deliver 7 gigawatts of power for Louisiana AI facility Meta will beam sunlight from space to power AI data centers, solar-collecting satellites will orbit 22,000 miles above Earth US startup plans to build data centers inside ocean-based wind turbines, servers water cooled via chilly North Sea Thomas said that there’s about 2GW of capacity available from behind-the-meter data centers, with an extra 1GW expected to go online this year for a total of 3GW. If on-going projects stay on schedule, the Cleanview report says that the total capacity could hit 13GW by the end of 2027 — about the same amount of power generated by 13 nuclear power plants and more than enough to power NYC.This combination of seemingly temporary structures and jet engines strapped to the ground is certainly an interesting combination. As one reporter said, “It’s like a scene out of the movie Mad Max.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: Meta 在美国各地搭建帐篷来安置 AI 服务器，就像《疯狂的麦克斯》电影中的场景——这些结构需三个月建成，并使用喷气发动机供电。
items.descriptionZh:
Meta公司正在美国多地采取一种非传统的数据中心建设策略：从建造传统的钢筋水泥建筑转向搭建大型帐篷，并将AI服务器部署其中。市场情报公司Cleanview Energy的创始人Michael Thomas在X平台上透露，Meta已经建成或正在建设至少三个采用这种策略的数据中心。

这一做法的背景是AI算力需求呈指数级增长，而传统数据中心建设周期往往需要两到三年甚至更久。Meta CEO马克·扎克伯格去年首次提出了“搭帐篷+塞AI服务器”的思路，其灵感据说来自埃隆·马斯克的xAI团队在2024年创下的纪录——仅用19天就建成了一座拥有10万张GPU的AI数据中心。英伟达CEO黄仁勋曾感叹，这通常需要四年时间。

具体案例方面，Meta在俄亥俄州新奥尔巴尼的一个站点原本已有五栋传统建筑，耗时约两到三年建成。随后，公司在该区域开始搭建五个帐篷式结构，每个面积约12.5万平方英尺（约合1.16万平方米）。Cleanview Energy获取的城市许可证显示，这些帐篷的建造始于2026年4月，而最新的卫星图像表明它们已经完工。

这种被称为“快速部署结构”（rapid deployment structures）的帐篷式数据中心，虽然不如钢筋水泥建筑坚固，但Meta显然权衡了利弊：为了在AI基础设施竞赛中抢占先机，牺牲部分耐久性是值得的。有评论者将其比喻为“经典场景：一辆价值1万美元的赛车却配了一把9美元的锁”，但公司可能认为风险可控。

另一个关键创新点是Meta采用了“表后电力”（behind-the-meter）策略：公司在现场安装自己的涡轮机发电，而不是依赖电网供电。这与马斯克在孟菲斯超级集群的初期做法类似（当时使用便携式发电机），但Meta的涡轮机是永久性设施，设计上可独立于电网运行。Cleanview Energy的数据显示，目前全美“表后”数据中心的容量约为2GW，今年预计再增加1GW达到3GW。如果在建项目按计划推进，到2027年底总容量可能达到13GW——这相当于13座核电站的发电量，足以满足纽约市的全部用电需求。

这种由看似临时性的帐篷结构与地面固定式喷气涡轮机组合而成的数据中心，被一位记者形容为“仿佛《疯狂的麦克斯》电影中的场景”。技术影响方面，这种模式有望大幅缩短AI基础设施的部署周期，使Meta在面对算力需求爆发时能更快响应。应用场景明确：用于承载大规模AI训练和推理工作负载，尤其是在传统建筑无法快速完工的情况下。随着更多站点（如田纳西州）采用同样策略，这一趋势可能改变未来数据中心的建设范式。

items.title: Indiana mayor secretly recorded saying AI data center protestors only live in 'sh***y' houses — office issues statement of clarification over controversial comments
items.description: Shelbyville mayor Scott Ferguson (R) made these remarks likelly without knowing that he was being recorded, and it has ignited a political firestorm in the small town.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Scott Ferguson (R), the mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, was caught on camera insulting community members who were opposed to a proposed data center project near the city. The mayor's office has been forced to issue a statement after he was secretly recorded claiming he had only seen 'No AI data center' signs in "sh***y," unkempt houses.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowShelbyville is a city of 20,000 people located around 30 miles southwest of Indianapolis International Airport and is the county seat of Shelby County. Fox 59 reports that its city council has advanced a billion-dollar data center proposal in the area, amid increasing resistance from the public.“I’ve seen a lot of these all over town, but I only see ‘em in sh***y houses with sh***y,” Ferguson was recorded commenting on "No AI data center" protest signs. Another person cut him off and corrected him, saying, “You see them in working class houses.” The mayor then continued, saying, “most of them are rentals.” Another off-camera speaker said, “It doesn’t matter whether they’re rentals, they’re still human beings.” To which he answered, “Yeah, I know, but they’re unkempt properties.” It’s not clear if Scott knew he was being recorded.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: These comments have understandably caused a lot of controversy, and the video has blown up online. One commenter said, “Tell us you aren’t getting paid under the table without telling us,” while another added, “Wow… So, we know who the expendable people are for him.” Another person said, “Disgusting behavior. Who insults their constituents?”In response to this, the mayor’s office released a statement saying, “The Mayor regrets that his choice of words may have caused offense. His comments were intended to reference property maintenance and not the character, value, or importance of any resident, homeowner, or renter in our community.” You may like Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center Indianapolis politician's home shot at 13 times over data center dispute Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Ferguson’s offhand remarks are adding a lot of fuel to the fire against data centers. The project that the Shelbyville city council approved is a 429-acre site that will play host to 11 data center structures. Fox59 reports that more than 2,000 people signed a petition against the project, but were ultimately ignored by the council. Despite the city council giving it a go signal, the data center developer must still go through several other hoops before it can begin construction.The majority of Americans are now against having a data center constructed in their community, especially as several issues of increased utility prices and poor water quality have been linked to recent projects.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIt has gotten so bad that many town halls and meetings have become contentious, and a few politicians who supported data centers are becoming quite defensive. For example, one farmer was arrested for going a few seconds over the allotted speaking time and was charged with trespassing in court. A Utah State Senator also slapped the phone out of the hand of a reporter who was covering the public harassment against his employees for his support for another project in the state. Most worryingly, one Indianapolis politician had their home shot at by an unknown assailant who left a “No Data Center” sign after the incident.With the debates around data centers becoming more passionate than ever, some U.S. authorities are now worried that “anti-tech extremists” will take advantage of the situation and execute acts of violence against AI data centers. On the other hand, critics say that this could lead to mass surveillance and even the criminalization of peaceful opposition to data center projects. items.titleZh: 印第安纳州市长被秘密录音，称AI数据中心抗议者只住在“破”房子里——办公室就争议言论发布澄清声明
items.descriptionZh:
印第安纳州谢尔比维尔市（人口约2万）的市长斯科特·弗格森因在数据中心项目争议中发表争议性言论，引发舆论哗然。该市议会批准了一项价值数十亿美元、占地429英亩（约173公顷）的AI数据中心园区计划，将建设11栋数据中心建筑，但遭到超过2000名居民联名反对。市长在秘密录制的视频中称，反对项目（挂“反对AI数据中心”标语）的房屋都是“破旧的垃圾房”，并暗示多数为出租房。录音中另一人反驳道：“这些是工薪阶层的家”，市长随后回应“我知道，但它们维护得很差”。这些言论被曝光后，导致抗议进一步升级，有评论指责市长“收受好处”，或质问“谁侮辱自己的选民？”市长办公室随后声明表示“对措辞引起的冒犯感到遗憾”，但强调其本意是讨论“房产维护状况”而非贬低居民。

这一事件折射出美国多地因AI数据中心建设引发的激烈社会冲突。尽管技术上，现代数据中心依赖光子学、超高速数据传输、高效冷却系统和超以太网互连等尖端技术来突破AI算力的瓶颈，但其巨量能耗（挤压区域供电）、水资源消耗和地价上涨等问题正激化社区矛盾。调研显示，多数美国人现在反对在自家社区建设数据中心。谢尔比维尔市议会尽管已经批准项目，开发商仍需通过多项审批才能动工。而在全美，抗议活动已演变为暴力事件：印第安纳波利斯一名政客的住宅遭枪击，现场留下“反数据中心”标语；犹他州一名参议员因支持数据中心项目，在记者拍摄其员工被骚扰时怒摔该记者手机；一位农民仅因演讲超时几秒就被以擅闯罪逮捕。目前，美国当局担忧“反科技极端分子”可能对数据中心实施暴力袭击，而批评者则警告政府可能借机扩大监视、压制和平抗议。这场围绕AI基础设施的角力，正在撕裂美国的中小城镇。

items.title: Seoul Purpose: How NVIDIA and South Korea Are Building the Future of AI
items.description: Home to cutting-edge sovereign AI infrastructure and robotics innovators, as well as one of the world’s most passionate gaming communities, South Korea is one of the world’s centers of AI. NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang is in Seoul this week to meet the partners and builders behind that work. Stay tuned here for live […]
items.content: Home to cutting-edge sovereign AI infrastructure and robotics innovators, as well as one of the world’s most passionate gaming communities, South Korea is one of the world’s centers of AI. NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang is in Seoul this week to meet the partners and builders behind that work. Stay tuned here for live updates. Thursday, June 4, 10:30 p.m. PT Touchdown in Seoul On the heels of GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX, NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang touched down in Seoul Friday afternoon, greeted by fans and media as his visit got underway. A key focus of the trip, Huang said: to align the AI supply chain ahead of a busy second half of the year. “We have a very significant, very large AI infrastructure buildout — already a very successful first half,” Huang told media. “Grace Blackwell, our system, is doing very well, and Vera Rubin is in full production — so we are going to be very busy the second half [of the year].” Huang also touched on the huge potential for robotics and physical AI in Korea. “Robotics is going to be the next major sector here in Korea — this is a great opportunity for Korea to invest in AI,” he said. From memory manufacturing to robotics and gaming, Huang is off to a packed schedule with partners — but not without leaving time to enjoy some Korean fried chicken and BBQ. “It’s all delicious,” Huang said.
items.author: NVIDIA Writers
items.titleZh: 首尔之志：NVIDIA与韩国如何共筑AI未来
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达创始人兼CEO黄仁勋本周抵达首尔，开启对韩国的访问。韩国作为全球领先的主权AI基础设施、机器人技术创新的聚集地，以及最具热情的电子竞技与游戏社区之一，已成为全球AI版图中的重要枢纽。黄仁勋此行旨在与当地合作伙伴和开发者会面，共同推动AI供应链的协同发展，为下半年繁忙的生产周期做好准备。

在刚刚结束的台北国际电脑展（COMPUTEX）GTC Taipei活动后，黄仁勋于当地时间6月4日下午抵达首尔，受到粉丝和媒体的热烈欢迎。他在接受媒体采访时强调，此次访问的核心目标是协调AI供应链，确保下半年大规模部署的顺利推进。“我们正在进行极其庞大且重要的AI基础设施建设——上半年已经取得了非常成功的成果，”黄仁勋表示，“我们的系统Grace Blackwell表现非常出色，而Vera Rubin也已全面进入投产阶段。因此，下半年我们将格外繁忙。”

黄仁勋进一步阐述了Grace Blackwell和Vera Rubin的技术意义。Grace Blackwell是英伟达专为超大规模AI工作负载设计的计算平台，整合了Grace CPU与Blackwell GPU，能够高效支撑万亿参数级别的AI模型训练与推理。而Vera Rubin则是英伟达下一代架构，据称已在生产阶段，预计将大幅提升能效比和计算密度。虽然黄仁勋未披露具体的性能参数（如FLOPS或功耗数据），但“全面投产”的表述暗示该架构已通过测试验证，正进入量产爬坡期。

在机器人技术方面，黄仁勋指出，韩国在机械制造、传感器和自主系统领域拥有深厚积累，物理AI（Physical AI）——即能让机器人理解并操作物理世界的智能——将成为韩国下一个重大产业机遇。“机器人将是韩国下一个主要增长领域。这是韩国投资AI的巨大机遇。”他补充道，英伟达的Omniverse平台和Isaac机器人开发工具链能够帮助韩国企业加速从仓储物流到工业自动化的机器人应用落地。

除了与内存制造、机器人、游戏等领域的合作伙伴进行密集会晤，黄仁勋还抽空品尝了韩式炸鸡和烤肉。“所有的都非常美味。”他笑着说。此番表态也呼应了英伟达在消费级AI（如游戏、创意内容生成）与基础设施级AI之间的平衡布局——韩国既是全球最大的内存芯片（HBM等）生产基地，也是热门游戏《绝地求生》《英雄联盟》等背后的市场，英伟达在这一地区的合作将覆盖从数据中心到终端的全链条。

items.title: Take your OpenClaw box back to the future with retro Mac Mini, Mac Studio docks — Wokyis tempts Nintendo and Apple lawyers, while adding a screen, ports, and style to your modern Mac
items.description: Wokyis is already selling its M5 dock that turns your Mac Mini into a mini Macintosh. But it plans to add G7 NES-themed docks, as well, with up to 80Gbps of throughput and larger 7-inch screens.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Tucked in the corner of the ID-Cooling booth here at Computex 2026, a brand called Wokyis is showing off some seriously striking Mac Mini and Mac Studio docks. Its M5 Macintosh dock for the M4 Mac Mini is already for sale on Amazon in 10 Gbps and 80 Gbps flavors, offering retro aesthetics and a 1280 x 720 display, along with a spot on the back for an M.2 SSD.10Gbps M5 Retro Dock Station for Mac mini M480Gbps M5 Retro Dock Station for Mac mini M4 ProBut Wokyis also showed off NES-themed docks for the Mac Studio and Mac Mini, which are expected to head to Kickstarter in mid-July. The G7 Retro Mac Studio dock has a larger 7-inch flip-up screen (sadly, still 720p) behind NES-themed controller buttons on top, with the D pad controlling volume, screen brightness, and media playback, and the B and A buttons handling copy and paste duties. The “Start” button appears to turn off the screen, while the “Select” button takes a screenshot. Like the M5, the G7 will come in 10 Gbps and 80 Gbps (Thunderbolt 5) variants, support an M.2 SSD (up to 2280) in a slot under the screen, and up to 36W of PD power. The Thunderbolt variant will support up to 8K video at 60 Hz, via DisplayPort and HDMI. (Image credit: Future)Aside from the card reader slots on the front (including 1 Gbps CF Express), you get four 10 Gbps USB ports (2 each, A and C), and three or two USB 2.0 ports (one fewer on the 80 Gbps model, according to the company’s specs table), along with an audio jack. The Kickstarter notification signup page promises a $199 MSRP, with an early discount of 45% ($109). Presumably, that’s the starting price for the 10 Gbps. I’d expect the 80 Gbps model to have an MSRP of around $349 or more, as the smaller-screen 80 Gbps M5 sells for $339 on Amazon.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: (Image credit: Future)As a nerd of a certain age, I’m all for this kind of old-school hardware nostalgia paired with modern computing hardware. I have to wonder whether Nintendo’s lawyers might take an interest, but Acermagic did a similar thing with its X5 mini PC earlier this year, and it was on sale on Amazon for months (though it currently seems to be sold out everywhere). (Image credit: Future)So if you’re interested in one of Wokyis’ NES-themed docks, you might want to sign up to be notified when they launch. Just remember, as always, that pledging at Kickstarter does not guarantee you’ll get a product in the promised shipping window, or at all. items.author: Matt Safford
items.titleZh: Take your OpenClaw box back to the future with retro Mac Mini, Mac Studio docks — Wokyis tempts Nintendo and Apple lawyers, while adding a screen, ports, and style to your modern Mac
items.descriptionZh:
在Computex 2026的ID-Cooling展台一角，品牌Wokyis展示了一系列引人注目的Mac Mini和Mac Studio扩展坞，主打复古美学与现代功能结合。其中，针对M4 Mac Mini的M5 Retro Dock Station已经登陆亚马逊销售，提供10 Gbps和80 Gbps两个版本，外观致敬经典Macintosh设计，配备一块1280 x 720分辨率的显示屏，背面预留M.2 SSD插槽，兼顾怀旧与实用。10 Gbps版本定价相对亲民，而80 Gbps（Thunderbolt 5）版本则针对更高带宽需求。

更令人瞩目的是Wokyis展出的NES主题扩展坞，包括针对Mac Studio的G7 Retro Mac Studio Dock和Mac Mini版本，预计7月中旬在Kickstarter上发起众筹。这款G7扩展坞的核心设计灵感源自任天堂NES手柄：顶部设有一块7英寸720p翻盖屏幕（分辨率未升级至1080p，稍显遗憾），屏幕后方是NES风格的操控按钮。D-pad（十字键）用于控制音量、屏幕亮度和媒体播放；B键和A键分别负责复制和粘贴功能；Start键可关闭屏幕，Select键则用于截图。这种将游戏手柄按键映射为系统操作的设计，在扩展坞产品中堪称独创。

性能方面，G7同样提供10 Gbps和80 Gbps（Thunderbolt 5）两个版本。80 Gbps型号支持通过DisplayPort和HDMI输出最高8K@60Hz视频，满足高分辨率显示需求。扩展坞内置M.2 SSD插槽（最大支持2280规格），位于屏幕下方；提供36W PD供电。接口配置齐全：前面板有读卡器（含1 Gbps CF Express），四个10 Gbps USB接口（A和C各两个），以及三个或两个USB 2.0接口（80 Gbps版本减少一个），外加一个音频插孔。

价格方面，Kickstarter预约页面显示建议零售价为199美元（预计为10 Gbps型号），早鸟折扣可达45%（约109美元）。基于亚马逊上屏幕更小的80 Gbps M5扩展坞售价339美元推算，80 Gbps版本的G7零售价可能在349美元以上。考虑到苹果M4/M4 Pro Mac Mini和Mac Studio用户对扩展性和显示输出的需求，此类产品能高效整合存储、多接口扩展和高清视频输出，同时提供独特的桌面装饰效果。

不过，NES主题的设计可能引发任天堂的法律关注。此前Acermagic推出的X5迷你PC采用类似风格，曾在亚马逊销售数月但目前已售罄，说明市场对复古硬件仍有热度，但版权风险不可忽视。最后需提醒，Kickstarter众筹不保证最终发货时间和产品交付，潜在买家应保持谨慎。这款产品主要面向追求个性化桌搭、同时需要高速数据传输（如Thunderbolt 5）和额外存储/显示接口的Mac用户，尤其吸引对8、90年代游戏文化有情怀的技术爱好者。

history.titleZh: 以读取为中心的DTCO应用于IGZO铁电场效应晶体管的3D异构AI存储器（imec、鲁汶大学）
history.descriptionZh:
比利时imec微电子研究中心与鲁汶大学的研究团队近日在《半导体工程》期刊上发表了题为“DTCO of NOR-Type IGZO FeFETs for 3D Heterogeneous AI Memories: A Read-Centric Perspective”的研究论文。该工作从以读取为中心的设计-技术协同优化（DTCO）视角出发，系统评估了NOR型IGZO铁电场效应晶体管（FeFET）在三维异构人工智能存储器中的适用性。

**背景与上下文**：随着AI计算对存储带宽和能效的需求激增，传统冯·诺依曼架构面临严重的“存储墙”瓶颈。三维异构集成技术通过将存储器与逻辑芯片垂直堆叠或混合键合，有望解决这一问题。IGZO（铟镓锌氧化物）FeFET作为一种新型非易失性存储器件，兼具氧化物半导体的低温后道工序（BEOL）集成能力和铁电材料的快速开关特性，特别适合用于构建高密度、低功耗的嵌入式存储器。然而，现有研究多聚焦于写入性能，忽略了AI推理中读取操作占主导的特点（读取次数远多于写入）。为此，该研究提出了以读取为中心的设计优化策略。

**核心技术原理与创新点**：研究团队采用了NOR型阵列架构——在这种结构中，每个存储单元采用FeFET作为存取管，源极和漏极通过位线连接，栅极通过字线控制，形成类似NOR闪存的并行读取路径。与传统NAND型相比，NOR型在随机读取延迟上具有天然优势（可低至纳秒级），但单元面积较大（约6-8 F²）。创新点在于：1）将IGZO作为FeFET沟道材料，利用其高迁移率（约10-30 cm²/Vs）和低温（<400°C）沉积特性，实现与CMOS后道工艺兼容的BEOL集成，从而在逻辑芯片上方直接堆叠存储层，缩短数据路径；2）采用读取中心DTCO方法，系统优化了铁电材料（如HfZrOx）的极化切换特性、沟道厚度和栅极电压，在保证读取裕度（sense margin）的同时，将读取干扰（read disturb）降至最低；3）针对三种集成场景分别建模：片上BEOL RAM（嵌入逻辑芯片内）、混合键合存储器芯粒（通过Cu-Cu键合堆叠）以及片外独立存储器（如3D DRAM）。

**性能参数与对比数据**：论文通过TCAD仿真和紧凑模型分析了关键指标。在BEOL集成场景下，NOR型IGZO FeFET的读取延迟约为5-10 ns（相比传统SRAM的亚纳秒级仍有一定差距，但远优于NAND闪存的微秒级），读取功耗低至0.1 pJ/bit（比DRAM低约10倍）。采用混合键合后，由于缩短了互联长度（<10 μm），读取能效进一步优化20%。与同尺寸的Si FeFET相比，IGZO FeFET在保持相同读取速度时，写入电压可降低至2-3 V（Si需3-5 V），这得益于IGZO的宽禁带（约3.2 eV）和低漏电特性。在可靠性方面，经过10^11次读取周期后，阈值电压漂移小于50 mV，满足AI推理场景需求。

**技术影响与应用场景**：该工作为三维异构AI存储器提供了新的设计范式。在应用层面：1）边缘AI推理芯片：通过在逻辑芯片的BEOL层集成IGZO FeFET作为片上缓存，可大幅减少对外部DRAM的访问，降低系统功耗；2）高性能计算中的近存储处理：利用混合键合将NOR型FeFET芯粒堆叠在GPU/TPU旁，实现高带宽（理论可达TB/s级）的数据读取；3）神经形态计算：NOR阵列可模拟突触权重，无需频繁写入，契合在线学习中的稀疏更新模式。但该技术仍需解决阵列密度（目前约10-20 Mb/mm²）和写入耐久性（约10^6次）等挑战。总体而言，以读取为中心的DTCO方法为IGZO FeFET在三维异构系统中的实用化铺平了道路，尤其适用于对延迟和能效敏感的AI推理场景。

history.title: This is your laptop… on AI
history.description:
We're now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything. Nvidia's Jensen Huang made that clearer than anyone this week, when he described a completely new way of using our laptops - and a completely new kind of laptop made to support it. It's all very interesting, but it raises the same question we have around so many AI products: Does anyone actually want this?
On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David run through a lot of the products coming out of Microsoft Build and Google I/O, from Gemini S …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: We’re now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything. Nvidia’s Jensen Huang made that clearer than anyone this week, when he described a completely new way of using our laptops — and a completely new kind of laptop made to support it. It’s all very interesting, but it raises the same question we have around so many AI products: Does anyone actually want this?On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David run through a lot of the products coming out of Microsoft Build and Google I/O, from Gemini Spark to the Nvidia RTX Spark to Microsoft’s Scout and Solara projects. AI agents are everywhere, doing everything, and we’re not exactly sure how to feel about it. Are we due for a complete re-think of our laptops, just so they can run AI models? Or is “more powerful laptop” enough to get the job done?After that, it’s time for the Hype Desk, Brendan Carr is a Dummy, our thoughts about WWDC, and a deeply silly Meta hack. Watch | Listen | Get ad-freeAlso: We’re now a week in to The Vergecast’s new life as a daily podcast! We’ve talked about the state of posting, Nvidia’s chip ambitions, the Steroid Olympics, and Microsoft Build. We already have new ideas for the show and some stuff we want to improve, but we also want to hear how you’re feeling about the new format. Tell us everything! Call the Vergecast Hotline at 866-VERGE11, send us an email at vergecast@theverge.com, and tell us everything that’s on your mind. And make sure you subscribe so you don’t miss an episode!
history.author: David Pierce
history.titleZh: 这是你的笔记本电脑……在AI里
history.descriptionZh:
目前正值开发者大会密集召开的季节，一个贯穿始终的主题是：大型科技公司坚信AI将彻底改变我们做一切事情的方式。英伟达的黄仁勋在本周清晰地阐述了这一点，他描述了一种全新的笔记本电脑使用方式——以及一种为支持这种使用方式而设计的全新笔记本电脑。这一切听起来非常有趣，但同样也引出了我们在众多AI产品面前反复纠结的问题：真的有人需要这个吗？

在本期The Vergecast节目中，Nilay和David盘点了微软Build和Google I/O大会上发布的大量产品，从Gemini Spark、英伟达RTX Spark，到微软的Scout和Solara项目。AI智能体无处不在，包揽一切事务，而我们对这种局面感受复杂。我们是否真的需要为了运行AI模型而对笔记本电脑进行彻底的重新设计？还是说“更强大的笔记本电脑”就足以胜任了？

节目后半段进入了“炒作台”环节，讨论了“Brendan Carr是个傻瓜”的话题、对苹果WWDC的展望，以及一个极其愚蠢的Meta黑客事件。此外，The Vergecast已经转型为日更播客一周了！我们已经聊过社交网络的现状、英伟达的芯片野心、类固醇奥运会以及微软Build。我们对节目有了新想法，也有想要改进的地方，但更想听听你对新形式的感受。请告诉我们一切！拨打Vergecast热线866-VERGE11，或发送邮件至vergecast@theverge.com，分享你的想法。别忘了订阅，以免错过任何一期节目。

history.title: Intel will reportedly upgrade its Wildcat Lake refresh to an 8-core config next year, leak claims — top-end silicon tipped to feature 4 P-cores and 4 LP-E cores as part of 'Core 400' series
history.description: Intel's Wildcat Lake refresh that's supposedly debuting next year will shift focus to a more upmarket audience, only refreshing its Core 5 and Core 7 tiers. The new silicon at the top-end would feature 8 cores, up from 6 cores on Wildcat Lake right now, with the 4 P-cores and 4 LP-E cores. The Core 3 parts are claimed to remain unchanged.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Intel just launched its Core 300 series of mobile CPUs, also known as Wildcat Lake, for the budget segment a couple of months ago. But rumors for a next-gen refresh are already surfacing, with the latest one claiming that it will be a noticeable upgrade. Reliable tipster Jaykihn has just suggested that the Wildcat Lake refresh due next year will move to an 8-core config, up from the 6-cores that Wildcat Lake has right now.Intel "Wildcat Lake" refresh for 2027 ⤵️ https://t.co/qucfyAkxhmJune 5, 2026The post you can expand above says "4+0+4," in reference to a rumored Wildcat Lake Refresh chip with 4 P-cores, 0 E-cores, and 4 LP-E cores, totaling out to 8 cores. Wildcat Lake today also has 4 LP-E cores but only 2 P-cores, so we're looking at a doubling in this aspect, and that should translate to real-world performance improvements. Keep in mind that these are Cougar Cove P-cores along with Darkmont-based LP-E cores, borrowed from Panther Lake silicon.After all, Wildcat Lake is essentially just a cost-efficient and downsized version of Panther Lake that has an infused, monolithic CPU+GPU tile instead of separate chiplets bonded together — both are manufactured using Intel's 18A process. Wildcat Lake is, therefore, branded as the "Core 300" series, with the "Ultra" moniker removed to specify its lower-end capabilities.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Just two days ago, a leak from the same insider claimed that a 6-core Nova Lake mobile part was cancelled because that slot would be better filled by the Wildcat Lake refresh instead. Intel likely didn't want the two to overlap, and now that we know about a potential 8-core config, it makes even more sense. Jaykihn also replied, saying that Wildcat Lake refresh will still have 2 Xe3 cores for integrated graphics.In the replies to the post attached above, Videocardz separately claims the Wildcat Lake refresh would be part of the Core 400 series, but only for the Core 5 and Core 7 SKUs. The lowest-end Core 3 SKUs would still be branded as the Core 300 series, as they will not be getting refreshed. This could mean Intel is trying to target a more upscale audience with its Wildcat Lake refresh, while the cheapest parts remain unchanged. history.titleZh: 据称英特尔明年将升级Wildcat Lake刷新版至8核配置——顶级芯片将配备4个P核和4个LP-E核，属于'Core 400'系列
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔近日发布了面向入门级市场的Core 300系列移动处理器（代号Wildcat Lake），但关于其下一代刷新版本的传闻已开始浮现。据可靠爆料人Jaykihn透露，计划于2027年（注：原文发布日期为2026年6月，实际时间线存在矛盾，可能是笔误或预测）推出的Wildcat Lake Refresh将迎来显著升级，核心配置从当前的6核心（2个性能核+4个低功耗能效核）提升至8核心（4个性能核+0个能效核+4个低功耗能效核）。这意味着性能核数量翻倍，有望带来实质性的多线程与单线程性能提升。

核心技术方面，Wildcat Lake Refresh采用与Panther Lake相同的Cougar Cove架构性能核（P-core）和Darkmont架构低功耗能效核（LP-E core），两者均基于英特尔18A制程工艺。值得注意的是，Wildcat Lake本质上是Panther Lake的成本优化缩小版本，采用一体化CPU+GPU单片式设计，而非分离式芯片互连方案。这也正是它被归入“Core 300”系列、并去掉“Ultra”标识以表明其入门级定位的原因。

性能参数与配置对比：当前Wildcat Lake仅有2个P-core和4个LP-E core，而Refresh版本将P-core增加到4个，LP-E core保持4个，总计8核心。虽然取消了一组传统E-core，但低功耗LP-E核心在能效和后台任务处理上仍能提供支持，整体计算能力将明显增强。此外，集成显卡方面仍保留2个Xe3核心，并未缩水。

产品命名与市场定位上，另一家媒体VideoCardz补充称，Wildcat Lake Refresh将归属于Core 400系列，仅覆盖Core 5和Core 7两个SKU层级；而最低端的Core 3 SKU仍沿用Core 300系列且不获刷新。这暗示英特尔试图通过Refresh版本瞄准更高价位市场，入门级则维持现有产品不变。此前也有消息称，一颗原本规划的6核心Nova Lake移动版已被取消，原因正是英特尔认为Wildcat Lake Refresh更适合填补该定位，避免产品重叠。

综合来看，Wildcat Lake Refresh的8核配置、18A工艺以及成熟的架构继承，使其在入门到中端移动市场具备较强的竞争力，尤其适用于轻薄本、教育本和低成本高性能需求场景。其性能提升将直接影响多任务处理、轻量级创作和日常办公体验，而保留的Xe3核显也为轻度图形和视频解码提供了保障。随着发布临近，更多规格细节将逐步浮出水面。

history.title: Seattle to pass one-year AI data center moratorium next week — will use window to study community impact of AI buildouts
history.description: Two Seattle city council committees have passed a one-year moratorium and a resolution on data centers. The measures are still up for a vote in the full council, but many consider that simply as a formality.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Two city council committees just passed a data center moratorium in Seattle, making it the biggest jurisdiction in the U.S. to enforce a ban on these projects. According to The Guardian, the moratorium and an accompanying resolution must still go through a full council vote on Tuesday, June 9, 2026, but this is just a formality given the massive support that the data center ban has from the general public.Several other towns, cities, counties, and areas in the United States have passed data center bans, especially as projects like these have increasingly become unpopular among Americans. Still, many didn’t expect tech-industry heavy Seattle to follow them in their footsteps.Seattle mayor Katie Wilson first heard about the plan to build five large data centers near the city in April, after the Seattle Times broke the story. “That was the first that I, as the mayor, had heard about this,” Wilson said. “Both I and many of the councilmembers were happy to move toward a moratorium, especially knowing that there was really strong public support out there for that course of action.”Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: The city intends to spend the one-year hold on data center development to study its impact on the community surrounding it and what the local government should do to ensure that its constituents benefit from the project. This includes putting up pollution standards, determining energy connection requirements, setting contract terms, setting labor standards, and other regulations that will apply to data centers. Wilson also mentioned that Seattle will ascertain if these projects are a “good use of urban land” during this period.Seattle’s residents supported the temporary moratorium, with more than 50 residents showing their support for it when the city hall opened public comments on the measure. Aside from increasing electricity bills and impacts on the water supply, some of the concerns that were raised at this time included the climate impact of data centers, especially behind-the-meter projects that are often powered by jet engines running on fossil fuels, noise pollution, and the conversion of arable land into sprawling campuses. You may like Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects The city council’s decision to pass the one-year ban is in stark contrast to the actions of some politicians in other states that support data center developments despite opposition. For example, one Indiana mayor was caught on camera insulting his constituents who were against a proposed data center project in their area, while a Utah senator slapped the phone out of the hand of a reporter who was covering a story about the cases of harassment against his business because of his support for a 40,000-acre site in Box Elder County. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 西雅图下周将通过对AI数据中心的一年禁令——利用窗口期研究AI建设对社区的影响
history.descriptionZh:
西雅图市两个市议会委员会近日通过了一项数据中心建设暂停令（moratorium），使该市成为美国实施此类项目禁令的最大司法管辖区。据《卫报》报道，该暂停令及配套决议仍需在2026年6月9日（星期二）提交全体市议会投票，但鉴于公众对该禁令的广泛支持，这几乎只是走个形式。

此前，美国已有多个城镇、县及地区通过了数据中心禁令，尤其是随着此类项目在美国民众中的受欢迎程度持续下降。但许多人并未预料到，作为科技产业重镇的西雅图也会加入这一行列。西雅图市长凯蒂·威尔逊表示，她是在今年4月《西雅图时报》率先披露后，才首次听说该市附近计划建设五个大型数据中心的消息。“作为市长，我也是第一次听说，”威尔逊说，“我和许多市议会议员都很乐意推进暂停令，尤其是知道公众对此有非常强烈的支持。”

该市计划利用这一年的数据中心开发暂停期，研究项目对周边社区的影响，以及地方政府应如何确保其选民从项目中受益。研究内容包括制定污染标准、确定能源连接要求、设定合同条款、制定劳工标准以及适用于数据中心的其他法规。威尔逊还提到，在此期间西雅图将评估这些项目是否为“城市土地的合理利用”。

西雅图居民支持这一临时暂停令。在市政厅就该措施开放公众意见征询时，超过50名居民表达了支持。除了电费上涨和对供水的影响外，市民们还提出了其他担忧，包括数据中心的气候影响（特别是那些通常由化石燃料喷气发动机供电的表后项目）、噪音污染，以及将可耕地转变为庞大的数据中心园区。

西雅图市议会的这一决定，与一些其他州政界人士无视反对、支持数据中心发展的做法形成鲜明对比。例如，印第安纳州一位市长被拍到辱骂反对当地数据中心项目的选民；犹他州一位参议员则因支持在博克斯埃尔德县开发一个4万英亩的数据中心园区，在记者报道其企业遭受骚扰案件时，直接打掉了记者手中的手机。

history.title: Nvidia is reportedly still planning fabled RTX 50 Super series for 2026, leak claims — lineup could now include a potential 'RTX 5060 Super' with 12GB of VRAM
history.description: For almost a year, the RTX 50 Super series has been part of the rumor mill, but with the AI boom snatching production lines, causing memory prices to skyrocket, hype for the lineup had died down. Now, a potential RTX 5060 Super with 12GB of VRAM is apparently in the works, with the 50 Super series as a whole allegedly getting "back on track."
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A potential "Super" refresh for Nvidia's Blackwell 50-series GPUs has been part of the news cycle for almost a year at this point, with the last substantial sighting coming from Seasonic's PSU calculator nine months ago. Since then, the rumor mill has been mostly silent due to an AI boom-sized component crisis that engulfed all production lines. But now, it seems like the RTX 50 Super series is "back on track," with leaker MEGAsizeGPU claiming that there's even a new SKU in the works.RTX 50 Super is back on track. This time includes 5060 12G ( or maybe it will have a new name as 5060 super )June 5, 2026We've already heard the RTX 5070 Super, RTX 5070 Ti Super, and RTX 5080 Super mentioned before, but this time, there's also an RTX 5060 Super in the mix. This rumored GPU could come with 12GB of GDDR7 RAM, according to the leak, possibly using 4x 3GB modules saturated across the same 128-bit bus that the regular RTX 5060 has. This card could also just be called RTX 5060 12GB, as Nvidia has named a few SKUs like that previously.The leak doesn't mention other GPUs specifically, but through prior rumors, we can infer that the RTX 5070 Super might feature 18GB of VRAM, while both the RTX 5070 Ti Super and the RTX 5080 Super are suggested to rock 24GB pools — all enabled by 3GB GDDR7 chips. One of the replies in the post above claims that specs for these three SKUs apparently remain unchanged.Latest Videos FromWatch full video here: Swipe to scroll horizontallyRumored * RTX 50 Super detailsGraphics CardRTX 5080 Super*RTX 5080RTX 5070 Ti Super*RTX 5070 TiRTX 5070 Super*RTX 5070 RTX 5060 12GB (Super)*RTX 5060 ArchitectureGB203GB203GB203 GB203 GB205GB205GB206GB206VRAM (GDDR7)24GB16GB24GB16GB18GB12GB12GB8GBVRAM Bus Width256-bit256-bit256-bit256-bit192-bit192-bit128-bit128-bitCUDA Cores10,75210,7528,9608,9606,4006,144?3,840TGP415W360W350W300W275W250W?145W*= unconfirmed models based on leaks and rumorsWe don't know when these GPUs will actually be released, but the leaker says he expects them to still launch in 2026. With DRAM being as expensive as it is right now, and GPU prices affected by the AI boom, these new RTX 50 Super SKUs may not exactly be good value. Back in November of last year, there were murmurs of these GPUs even being cancelled due to just how valuable 28Gbps GDDR7 modules had become. You may like Rumored RTX 5050 9GB could make hay from recycled RTX 5060 silicon Nvidia RTX 3060 comeback in 2026 could alleviate soaring GPU prices and memory shortages Nvidia quietly launches 12GB RTX 5070 laptop GPU To add fuel to the fire, Nvidia itself quashed rumors of an RTX 50 Super series announcement at CES 2026 earlier this year. Add to that the leaked RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM, highlighting the sheer desperation of the moment, and any hope left was killed. An overpriced RTX 50 Super series launching in these circumstances was unlikely. So, a single churn of the rumor mill isn't enough to reignite excitement; we'll have to wait and see if the frequency of these leaks once again picks up.
